title: 1 FilterBoxBuilder: Architecture Overview
goal: Understand the browser-only app shape, repository boundaries, and the shared layout object that connects settings, previews, and exports.

foreshadows.suggestedText: The app shell loaded here owns browser state; the next tour follows that state through the workbench.
The layout facade introduced here becomes the input to laser, print, and preview flows later.

title: 1.1 FilterBoxBuilder: Workbench State and Browser Shell
goal: Trace how the browser workbench owns controls, preview mode, fabrication mode, and per-design memory.

foreshadows.suggestedText: The workbench keeps raw settings; the next tour shows how those become typed domain settings.

callbacks.suggestedText: Recall that index.html loads main.ts directly; this tour starts inside that browser module.

title: 1.2 FilterBoxBuilder: Domain Settings and Presets
goal: Understand filter presets, fan product presets, print design presets, raw settings, normalized settings, and URL settings.

foreshadows.suggestedText: Corsi and donut values stored here get their own model builders in the next tour.
The normalized settings object here is what fabrication code receives later.

callbacks.suggestedText: The URL state from the workbench becomes the raw settings decoded in this tour.

title: 1.3 FilterBoxBuilder: Design Models
goal: Understand the generated Nukit geometry, the Corsi-Rosenthal model, and the donut HEPA adapter model.

foreshadows.suggestedText: The dimensions and face roles here are the inputs that become panels and assemblies.
The renderer later chooses different builders for Nukit, Corsi, donut, and static designs.

callbacks.suggestedText: The normalized settings from the previous tour are the input to these geometry functions.

title: 2 FilterBoxBuilder: Fabrication Pipeline and Laser Panels
goal: Follow normalized settings into layout results, laser panel drafts, sheet documents, assembly parts, and export diagnostics.

foreshadows.suggestedText: The cut sheet document is rendered through the Boxes port in the next tour.
These panel drafts are also the source for printable kit splitting.

callbacks.suggestedText: The geometry from the previous tour is where panel dimensions come from.

title: 2.1 FilterBoxBuilder: Boxes.py Port and SVG Output
goal: Understand the small Boxes.py-inspired drawing layer, edge vocabulary, SVG layers, and audit scripts.

foreshadows.suggestedText: The test tour shows how this port is checked against expected SVG and panel behavior.

callbacks.suggestedText: The document produced by fabrication is the same document rendered to SVG here.

title: 2.2 FilterBoxBuilder: Printable Kits and 3MF Export
goal: Trace print-volume presets, panel tiling, sheet planning, design routing, and 3MF package creation.

foreshadows.suggestedText: The sheet plan created here is rendered as printer plates in the preview tour.

callbacks.suggestedText: The panel list created in the fabrication tour is split into printable parts here.

title: 3 FilterBoxBuilder: Three.js Previews and Static Assets
goal: Understand assembled previews, print-sheet previews, static STL loading, vendor asset metadata, and scale references.

foreshadows.suggestedText: The final tour shows the renderer helpers that can be tested without browser screenshots.

callbacks.suggestedText: The print sheet plan from the previous tour is the main input to PrintSheetThreePreview.

title: 4 FilterBoxBuilder: Tests, Scripts, and Contributor Workflow
goal: Map tests, scripts, config, and docs to the app boundaries so contributors know where to add coverage.

callbacks.suggestedText: The workbench state from the app tour has direct unit tests here.
The Boxes port from the port tour is checked by equivalence tests and audit scripts here.
